etymology.description: semantic 土(土) (tǔ, "earth") + phonetic 其(其) (qí) -> a foundation of earth
etymology.interpretation: The character 基(jī, "foundation") combines the semantic component 土(土) (tǔ, "earth") with the phonetic component 其(其) (qí). The earth component suggests the idea of being on or in the ground, which is foundational for building. The phonetic part 其 provides the pronunciation but does not contribute to the meaning.

examples.chinese: 这个建筑的基础很牢固(這個建築的基礎很牢固)。
examples.pinyin: Zhège jiànzhù de jīchǔ hěn láogù.
examples.english: The foundation of this building is very solid.

examples.chinese: 他用数学为他的理论奠定了基础(他用數學為他的理論奠定了基礎)。
examples.pinyin: Tā yòng shùxué wèi tā de lǐlùn diàndìngle jīchǔ.
examples.english: He laid the foundation for his theory using mathematics.

examples.chinese: 教育是社会进步的基础(教育是社會進步的基礎)。
examples.pinyin: Jiàoyù shì shèhuì jìnbù de jīchǔ.
examples.english: Education is the foundation of societal progress.
